title: Toji with one leg
wikitext:
[]: Toji with one leg             Quote Post by xPearse » Wed Jul 09, 2014 9:40 am I just discovered this in one of the unofficial artbooks and damn does that look nasty, I mean like ouch. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 09, 2014 9:51 am You can tell that he's missing a leg in the show when Hikari visits him the hospital. It's easy to miss, but you can see from the way the bed sheets are laying that he only has one leg. They were supposed to put him in The End of Evangelion briefly, where it would have been more clear: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2163/Toji-was-in-storyboards-for-EoE-Whats-the-context/

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Wed Jul 09, 2014 10:18 am Yea I know he lost it, they hid that well. I was just surprised to see this pic.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 09, 2014 10:33 am Jesus, look at Asuka. What's the caption for the picture? ARE YOU NOT ENTERTAINED?

[]:             Quote Post by Firetrout » Wed Jul 09, 2014 10:36 am Why does Shinji have an afro?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jul 09, 2014 10:38 am He ran his hands through his hair after the previous scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jul 11, 2014 12:20 am But does he still have both of his testicles?

[]:             Quote Post by goodnight humanitarian » Fri Jul 11, 2014 12:48 am It is interesting that Shinji and Asuka are trying to preserve their modesty while Toji and Rei stand without any attempt to hide their hurt/disabled bodies. Maybe it is just an artistic choice or maybe it is a statement trying to pair up two juxapositions (Shinji/Asuka as the passive boy/boisterous girl and Rei/Toji as the passive girl/boisterous boy). I'm probably reading too much into it, but it does make me wonder what the group dynamics would have been if Toji had a longer career piloting the eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Fri Jul 11, 2014 6:32 am Argh. That picture's just brutal. Especially since they give the kids realistic proportions for their age. It looks like something you'd expect to see in a brutal expose about child soldiers on AlJazeera.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Sat Jul 12, 2014 5:28 am This is one of the best pieces of non-canon Eva-art, I've come across. May somebody tell me, where this is from and who has drawn it?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Jul 12, 2014 8:21 am The doujin is called Eva-Fix from the circle Chocolate Shop and was published in 1997. The artist for that specific page is Choco, who eventually did work his way from doujins and into the industry: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/people.php?id=116161 This doujin is particularly annoying because people keep confusing some of its art for Yamashita's.
